programs.title: Child Allowance (児童手当 / Jidō Teate)
programs.authority: Children and Families Agency (こども家庭庁 / Kodomo Katei-chō); administered by municipalities
programs.summary: National cash allowance paid to a person raising a child resident in Japan, intended to support child-rearing households. Paid periodically (multiple times per year) through the family's municipality. Coverage and the per-child structure have been revised over time (notably an expansion effective late 2024), so scope and ages should be confirmed for the current period.
programs.eligibilityNote: Generally for a resident raising an eligible child; conditions and any income-related rules have changed over time. Eligibility is NOT determined here — confirm with the municipality and the Children and Families Agency.
programs.legalBasis: Child Allowance Act (児童手当法)
programs.provenance: https://www.cfa.go.jp/policies/kokoseido/jidouteate ; corroborated via municipal multilingual guides (e.g. city.komatsu.lg.jp, city.izumo.shimane.jp)

programs.title: Childcare Leave Benefit (育児休業給付金 / Ikuji Kyūgyō Kyūfukin)
programs.authority: Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare (厚生労働省 / MHLW); administered through Hello Work (ハローワーク) under Employment Insurance
programs.summary: Income-replacement benefit paid from Employment Insurance to an insured worker who takes childcare leave to raise a child. Administered via Hello Work (typically through the employer) and paid in installments during the leave period. A separate post-childbirth support benefit was added in 2025; details evolve, so confirm current structure.
programs.eligibilityNote: Generally for workers enrolled in Employment Insurance who take childcare leave and meet contribution-history conditions; rates and durations change. Eligibility is NOT determined here — confirm with Hello Work / MHLW and your employer.
programs.legalBasis: Act on Childcare Leave, Caregiver Leave, and Other Measures for the Welfare of Workers Caring for Children or Other Family Members (育児・介護休業法); Employment Insurance Act (雇用保険法)

programs.title: Free Early Childhood Education and Care (幼児教育・保育の無償化 / Yōji Kyōiku Hoiku no Mushōka)
programs.authority: Children and Families Agency (こども家庭庁) / Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology (MEXT); administered by municipalities
programs.summary: National measure that makes use of kindergartens, licensed nursery centres (hoikuen), and certified centres free or subsidized for children in qualifying age ranges, reducing the cost of early childhood education and care for families. Scope (ages, facility types, and treatment of unlicensed facilities) is defined by program rules administered by municipalities.
programs.eligibilityNote: Coverage depends on child's age, facility type, and usage; some categories require an application or have caps. Eligibility is NOT determined here — confirm with the municipality and the Children and Families Agency.
programs.legalBasis: Child and Childcare Support Act (子ども・子育て支援法)
programs.provenance: https://www.cfa.go.jp/policies/kokoseido/mushouka (Children and Families Agency free ECEC program page)

programs.title: Long-Term Care Insurance (介護保険 / Kaigo Hoken)
programs.authority: Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare (厚生労働省 / MHLW); operated by municipalities as insurers
programs.summary: Mandatory social-insurance system providing in-kind long-term care services (home care, day services, facility care, etc.) for people assessed as needing care, primarily older adults. After a needs-assessment (要介護認定) a care level is determined and a care plan gives access to covered services. Run by municipalities under national rules; everyone aged 40+ contributes.
programs.eligibilityNote: Generally available to insured persons aged 65+ (and 40–64 for certain specified conditions) who pass the needs-assessment; service access follows the assigned care level. Eligibility and assessment are NOT determined here — confirm with the municipality and MHLW.
programs.legalBasis: Long-Term Care Insurance Act (介護保険法)

programs.title: Special Child-Rearing Allowance (特別児童扶養手当 / Tokubetsu Jidō Fuyō Teate)
programs.authority: Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare (厚生労働省 / MHLW); administered by prefectures/municipalities
programs.summary: National cash allowance paid to a parent or guardian raising a child under 20 with a moderate-to-severe mental or physical disability at home, to support the added burden of care. Graded by disability level. A companion program, the Disability Child Welfare Allowance (障害児福祉手当), supports severely disabled children needing constant care.
programs.eligibilityNote: Generally for caregivers of a qualifying child under 20 with a recognized disability; income limits and grading apply. Eligibility is NOT determined here — confirm with the prefecture/municipality and MHLW.
programs.legalBasis: Act on the Payment of Special Child-Rearing Allowance, etc. (特別児童扶養手当等の支給に関する法律)

programs.title: Family Support Centre Programme (ファミリー・サポート・センター / Family Support Center)
programs.authority: Children and Families Agency (こども家庭庁) / Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare (MHLW); operated by municipalities
programs.summary: Community mutual-aid childcare scheme in which local members who want help with child-rearing are matched with members willing to provide help (e.g., temporary childcare, pickup/drop-off support, care during short gaps). Coordinated by a municipal Family Support Centre as a local child-rearing support service rather than a cash benefit.
programs.eligibilityNote: Generally open to families in the operating municipality who register; available support and any fees are set locally. Participation terms are NOT determined here — confirm with the local Family Support Centre / municipality.
programs.legalBasis: Operated under child and child-rearing support measures (子ども・子育て支援 関連事業); a community support service rather than a statutory cash entitlement

programs.title: Child Tax Credit (CTC)
programs.authority: U.S. Internal Revenue Service (IRS), Department of the Treasury
programs.summary: Federal tax credit that reduces a household's income-tax liability for each qualifying child under age 17, with a portion potentially refundable (Additional Child Tax Credit) for eligible lower-income families. The U.S. has no universal monthly child allowance; the CTC delivered via the income-tax system is the closest federal child-benefit equivalent. Claimed annually on the federal tax return (Form 1040, Schedule 8812).
programs.eligibilityNote: Generally requires a qualifying child with a valid SSN, dependency/residency/relationship tests, and is subject to income phase-outs; rules and amounts change with tax legislation. Eligibility is non-determinative here and must be confirmed with the IRS and/or a tax professional.
programs.legalBasis: Internal Revenue Code §24 (26 U.S.C. §24)

programs.title: Family and Medical Leave Act (FMLA) Leave
programs.authority: U.S. Department of Labor, Wage and Hour Division (federal employees: Office of Personnel Management)
programs.summary: Federal entitlement giving eligible employees of covered employers up to 12 workweeks of unpaid, job-protected leave in a 12-month period for the birth/adoption/foster placement of a child, to care for a family member with a serious health condition, the employee's own serious health condition, or certain military-family exigencies, with continuation of group health benefits. The U.S. has no national PAID parental/maternity leave program; FMLA is the federal job-protection floor (some states operate their own separate paid family-leave programs).
programs.eligibilityNote: Generally requires ~12 months and ~1,250 hours of service with a covered employer (50+ employees within 75 miles, plus public agencies and schools); leave is unpaid under federal law. Eligibility is non-determinative here — confirm with the U.S. Department of Labor and your employer.
programs.legalBasis: Family and Medical Leave Act of 1993 (29 U.S.C. §2601 et seq.); 29 CFR Part 825
programs.notes: hagukumi is a community CARE substrate (Liberation Ladder L4 Care, ADR-2605261030) — NOT a licensed care provider, NOT a benefits-determination service. This is an INFORMATIONAL directory of OFFICIAL public care-support programs (child + elder) for routing families to official support; it makes NO eligibility or benefit determination (eligibility + amounts vary by case and drift — confirm with the authority) and provides no care service here. UNVERIFIED SEED scaffold. FMLA is unpaid; separate state paid-family-leave programs exist and are out of scope for this federal entry.

programs.title: Child Care and Development Fund (CCDF) Subsidy
programs.authority: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services, Administration for Children and Families (ACF), Office of Child Care — administered by states, territories, and tribes
programs.summary: Federal-state block-grant program providing financial assistance to help low-income families pay for child care so parents can work, attend training, or pursue education. CCDF also supports child-care quality improvement. Subsidies are administered at the state/territory/tribal level under federal rules, so eligibility, provider rules, and copayments vary by jurisdiction.
programs.eligibilityNote: Generally targets lower-income working/training families with children (commonly under age 13), subject to state-set income limits and rules; available subsidy funds and waitlists vary. Eligibility is non-determinative here — confirm with your state/territory/tribal child-care subsidy agency.
programs.legalBasis: Child Care and Development Block Grant Act (42 U.S.C. §9857 et seq.); 45 CFR Part 98
programs.notes: hagukumi is a community CARE substrate (Liberation Ladder L4 Care, ADR-2605261030) — NOT a licensed care provider, NOT a benefits-determination service. This is an INFORMATIONAL directory of OFFICIAL public care-support programs (child + elder) for routing families to official support; it makes NO eligibility or benefit determination (eligibility + amounts vary by case and drift — confirm with the authority) and provides no care service here. UNVERIFIED SEED scaffold. Delivery and eligibility are state/territory/tribal — families apply through their local subsidy agency; childcare.gov is a useful federal locator.

programs.title: Medicaid Home & Community-Based Services (HCBS)
programs.authority: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services, Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS) — administered by state Medicaid agencies
programs.summary: Medicaid pathway letting eligible older adults and people with disabilities receive long-term services and supports (e.g., personal care, home health aide, adult day services, respite) in their own home or community rather than an institution. Offered chiefly through state 1915(c) waivers and related authorities, so covered services and eligibility differ by state. This is the principal U.S. public financing route for long-term elder care; Medicare's long-term-care coverage is limited.
programs.eligibilityNote: Generally requires Medicaid eligibility (income/asset limits) plus a functional/level-of-care need, with rules and waiver slots varying by state and possible waitlists. Eligibility is non-determinative here — confirm with your state Medicaid agency or local Area Agency on Aging.
programs.legalBasis: Social Security Act §1915(c) and related sections (42 U.S.C. §1396n)
programs.notes: hagukumi is a community CARE substrate (Liberation Ladder L4 Care, ADR-2605261030) — NOT a licensed care provider, NOT a benefits-determination service. This is an INFORMATIONAL directory of OFFICIAL public care-support programs (child + elder) for routing families to official support; it makes NO eligibility or benefit determination (eligibility + amounts vary by case and drift — confirm with the authority) and provides no care service here. UNVERIFIED SEED scaffold. Unlike many countries, the U.S. has no standalone long-term-care insurance; means-tested Medicaid HCBS is the primary public elder-care benefit.

programs.title: Social Security Disability Programs (SSDI & SSI)
programs.authority: U.S. Social Security Administration (SSA)
programs.summary: Two federal programs providing income support to people with qualifying disabilities: Social Security Disability Insurance (SSDI), based on prior work and payroll-tax contributions, and Supplemental Security Income (SSI), a needs-based program for aged, blind, or disabled people with limited income and resources (including children with disabilities). These cash benefits underpin care for disabled adults and children and often serve as a gateway to linked health coverage (Medicare/Medicaid).
programs.eligibilityNote: SSDI generally requires sufficient work credits and a medically determinable disability meeting SSA's definition; SSI is means-tested with strict income/resource limits. Eligibility is non-determinative here — confirm with the Social Security Administration.
programs.legalBasis: Social Security Act Title II (SSDI, 42 U.S.C. §401 et seq.) and Title XVI (SSI, 42 U.S.C. §1381 et seq.)
programs.notes: hagukumi is a community CARE substrate (Liberation Ladder L4 Care, ADR-2605261030) — NOT a licensed care provider, NOT a benefits-determination service. This is an INFORMATIONAL directory of OFFICIAL public care-support programs (child + elder) for routing families to official support; it makes NO eligibility or benefit determination (eligibility + amounts vary by case and drift — confirm with the authority) and provides no care service here. UNVERIFIED SEED scaffold. SSDI and SSI are distinct programs grouped here as the federal disability-care income supports; SSI explicitly covers disabled children.

programs.title: Head Start & Early Head Start
programs.authority: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services, Administration for Children and Families (ACF), Office of Head Start
programs.summary: Federally funded program promoting school readiness and family well-being for children from birth to age 5 in families with low income (and some other categories, e.g., children experiencing homelessness or in foster care). Head Start serves preschoolers ages 3–5; Early Head Start serves infants/toddlers from birth to age 3 and expectant families. Services span early learning, health screenings, nutrition, disability services, and family-support/engagement, delivered through local grantee agencies in centers, family child care, and home-based settings.
programs.eligibilityNote: Generally prioritizes families at or below federal poverty guidelines and certain categorical groups; availability depends on local grantee slots. Eligibility is non-determinative here — confirm with the Office of Head Start or your local Head Start program.
programs.legalBasis: Head Start Act (42 U.S.C. §9831 et seq.)

programs.title: Canada Child Benefit (CCB) / Allocation canadienne pour enfants (ACE)
programs.authority: Canada Revenue Agency (CRA) / Agence du revenu du Canada
programs.summary: A tax-free monthly payment made to eligible families to help with the cost of raising children under 18. The amount is income-tested (based on adjusted family net income from the prior tax year). May include related amounts for children eligible for the disability tax credit and is integrated with various provincial/territorial child benefit programs administered alongside it.
programs.eligibilityNote: Generally for the primary caregiver of a child under 18 who is resident in Canada and meets residency/status conditions; requires filing an annual tax return. Eligibility and amounts are case-specific and must be confirmed with the Canada Revenue Agency.
programs.legalBasis: Income Tax Act (Canada), Subdivision A.1 (Canada Child Benefit provisions)

programs.title: EI Maternity and Parental Benefits / Prestations de maternité et parentales de l'assurance-emploi
programs.authority: Employment and Social Development Canada / Service Canada (Employment Insurance program)
programs.summary: Employment Insurance income replacement for people away from work due to pregnancy/recent birth (maternity benefits) and for parents caring for a newborn or newly adopted child (parental benefits). Parental benefits can be taken as a standard or extended option, with extended providing a lower weekly rate over a longer period; parental weeks may be shared between two parents.
programs.eligibilityNote: Generally requires sufficient insurable employment hours and a qualifying interruption of earnings; maternity benefits are limited to the person giving birth. Quebec residents are covered instead by the Québec Parental Insurance Plan (QPIP/RQAP). Eligibility, week counts and rates are case-specific and must be confirmed with Service Canada.
programs.legalBasis: Employment Insurance Act (Canada)

programs.title: Canada-wide Early Learning and Child Care System (CWELCC, "$10-a-day child care") / Système pancanadien d'apprentissage et de garde des jeunes enfants
programs.authority: Employment and Social Development Canada (Federal Secretariat on Early Learning and Child Care), delivered via bilateral agreements with provinces and territories
programs.summary: A federal-provincial/territorial initiative reducing parent fees for regulated (licensed) early learning and child care toward an average of $10 a day and expanding the number of child care spaces. Fee reductions and access are delivered through participating licensed providers under each province's or territory's agreement; Quebec operates its own system with comparable federal support.
programs.eligibilityNote: Fee reductions apply to children enrolled in participating licensed/regulated child care in each jurisdiction; specifics (enrolment, age ranges, opted-in providers) vary by province/territory. Confirm participation and fees with the provincial/territorial child care authority and the specific licensed provider.
programs.legalBasis: Bilateral Canada-Wide Early Learning and Child Care agreements with provinces and territories (and related provincial/territorial legislation)
programs.notes: hagukumi is a community CARE substrate (Liberation Ladder L4 Care, ADR-2605261030) — NOT a licensed care provider, NOT a benefits-determination service. UNVERIFIED SEED scaffold. Delivery is jurisdiction-specific; the '$10-a-day' figure is the well-established program target, not a guaranteed individual rate.

programs.title: EI Caregiving Benefits (Family Caregiver and Compassionate Care) / Prestations de proche aidant de l'assurance-emploi
programs.authority: Employment and Social Development Canada / Service Canada (Employment Insurance program)
programs.summary: Employment Insurance income replacement for people who take time away from work to provide care or support to a critically ill or injured family member, or to someone needing end-of-life care (compassionate care). Includes distinct benefit types for caring for an adult, for a child, and compassionate care; weeks of benefits can be shared among eligible caregivers. Often used by working-age people supporting ageing or seriously ill relatives.
programs.eligibilityNote: Generally requires sufficient insurable employment hours, a qualifying interruption of earnings, and a medical certificate attesting that the person needs care/support; the caregiver need not live with or be biologically related to the person. Eligibility and week counts are case-specific and must be confirmed with Service Canada.
programs.legalBasis: Employment Insurance Act (Canada)
programs.notes: hagukumi is a community CARE substrate (Liberation Ladder L4 Care, ADR-2605261030) — NOT a licensed care provider, NOT a benefits-determination service. This is an INFORMATIONAL directory of OFFICIAL public care-support programs (child + elder) for routing families to official support; it makes NO eligibility or benefit determination (eligibility + amounts vary by case and drift — confirm with the authority) and provides no care service here. UNVERIFIED SEED scaffold. Canada has no single national long-term-care insurance like Japan's 介護保険; this EI benefit is the federal income-support analogue for caregivers, while long-term/home care services themselves are administered provincially. No weekly amounts asserted.

programs.title: Canada Disability Benefit (CDB) / Prestation canadienne pour les personnes handicapées
programs.authority: Employment and Social Development Canada / Service Canada
programs.summary: A federal income-support benefit intended to reduce poverty and support the financial security of working-age (18–64) persons with disabilities, supplementing provincial/territorial and other supports. The benefit is income-tested. Established by the Canada Disability Benefit Act with regulations setting out amounts and eligibility; payments commenced in 2025.
programs.eligibilityNote: Generally requires being aged 18–64, holding a valid Disability Tax Credit (DTC) certificate, meeting residency/status conditions, and having filed a tax return; the benefit is reduced based on income. Eligibility and amounts are case-specific and must be confirmed with Service Canada / the Canada Revenue Agency.
programs.legalBasis: Canada Disability Benefit Act, S.C. 2023, c. 17; Canada Disability Benefit Regulations (SOR/2025-35)

programs.title: Child Disability Benefit (CDB) / Prestation pour enfants handicapés (PEH)
programs.authority: Canada Revenue Agency (CRA) / Agence du revenu du Canada
programs.summary: A tax-free monthly amount paid to families who care for a child under 18 with a severe and prolonged impairment in physical or mental functions. It is paid as a supplement to the Canada Child Benefit and is income-tested; recipients of the CCB whose child qualifies for the disability tax credit are generally assessed for it automatically.
programs.eligibilityNote: Generally requires that the family be eligible for the Canada Child Benefit and that the child be eligible for the Disability Tax Credit (DTC) via an approved DTC application. Eligibility and amounts are case-specific and must be confirmed with the Canada Revenue Agency.
programs.legalBasis: Income Tax Act (Canada) (Child Disability Benefit supplement to the Canada Child Benefit)

programs.title: Welfare Pension for Older Adults / Pensión para el Bienestar de las Personas Adultas Mayores
programs.authority: Secretaría de Bienestar (Gobierno de México)
programs.summary: Universal non-contributory federal welfare pension paid bimonthly to people aged 65 and older resident in Mexico, deposited directly to a Banco del Bienestar card without intermediaries. Recognized as a constitutional right, so the State is obligated to deliver it regardless of the administration in office. Provides income support to older adults as part of the federal Programas para el Bienestar.
programs.eligibilityNote: Generally for people 65+ who are Mexican by birth or naturalization and resident in Mexico, presenting valid official ID. Eligibility, registration windows and amounts vary and drift — confirm with the authority.
programs.legalBasis: Constitución Política de los Estados Unidos Mexicanos, Art. 4 (constitutionalized welfare pensions); annual Reglas de Operación published in the DOF

programs.title: Welfare Pension for Persons with Permanent Disability / Pensión para el Bienestar de las Personas con Discapacidad Permanente
programs.authority: Secretaría de Bienestar (Gobierno de México)
programs.summary: Non-contributory federal welfare pension paid bimonthly to people with permanent disability, deposited directly to a Banco del Bienestar card. Coverage age range varies by state (universal 0–64 in states that signed universality agreements; otherwise focused on children, adolescents and young people 0–29, plus high-marginalization, indigenous and Afromexican localities). Constitutionalized like the older-adults pension.
programs.eligibilityNote: Generally for people with a certified permanent disability, with age and geographic coverage varying by state agreement. A medical/disability certificate is typically required. Eligibility, coverage and amounts vary and drift — confirm with the authority.
programs.legalBasis: Constitución Política de los Estados Unidos Mexicanos, Art. 4; annual Reglas de Operación published in the DOF

programs.summary: Statutory framework guaranteeing the rights of persons with disabilities through inclusion measures, affirmative action, and reasonable accommodation across health, education, social protection, work, transport and community life. programs.title: Child Benefit
programs.authority: HM Revenue & Customs (HMRC)
programs.summary: A regular payment to people responsible for bringing up a child under 16 (or under 20 if in approved education or training). One amount is paid for the eldest/only child and a lower amount for each additional child, paid every 4 weeks into a bank account. Claiming also provides National Insurance credits that protect the claimant's State Pension and helps the child receive a National Insurance number automatically. A High Income Child Benefit Charge can claw back some or all of the benefit where a claimant or partner has higher adjusted net income.
programs.eligibilityNote: Generally for a person responsible for a qualifying child living with them; subject to the High Income Child Benefit Charge at higher incomes. NON-determinative — eligibility, rates and the income charge thresholds must be confirmed with HMRC/GOV.UK.
programs.legalBasis: Social Security Contributions and Benefits Act 1992 (Part IX); Child Benefit (General) Regulations 2006

programs.title: Statutory Maternity / Paternity Pay and Shared Parental Leave (SPL)
programs.authority: GOV.UK / HM Revenue & Customs (HMRC) with employers; policy by Dept for Business and Trade
programs.summary: Statutory employment rights for new parents. Eligible birthing parents can take up to 52 weeks of maternity leave with up to 39 weeks of Statutory Maternity Pay; statutory paternity leave and pay are also available. Shared Parental Leave lets eligible parents share up to 50 weeks of leave and up to 37 weeks of pay between them in the first year after birth or adoption, including taking leave in separate blocks. Maternity Allowance is a related payment for those who do not qualify for Statutory Maternity Pay.
programs.eligibilityNote: Depends on employment status, continuous-service and earnings tests, and curtailing maternity/adoption leave to share it. NON-determinative — qualifying conditions, weeks and pay rates must be confirmed with GOV.UK/HMRC and the employer.
programs.legalBasis: Employment Rights Act 1996; Shared Parental Leave Regulations 2014; Statutory Maternity Pay (General) Regulations 1986; Maternity and Parental Leave etc. Regulations 1999

programs.summary: Two combined supports for childcare costs, applied for in one application. Tax-Free Childcare provides a government top-up into an online childcare account that parents use to pay registered providers (the government adds a proportion of what parents pay, up to a per-child quarterly cap). Government-funded childcare hours give eligible working parents in England a number of funded hours per week for young children up to school age (devolved nations operate their own funded-childcare schemes). Eligibility must be re-confirmed online periodically.

programs.title: Attendance Allowance
programs.authority: Department for Work and Pensions (DWP)
programs.summary: A non-means-tested benefit for people over State Pension age who have a disability or health condition severe enough that they need help with personal care or supervision. It is paid at two rates depending on the level of care needs (day and/or night needs). The claimant does not need to actually be receiving care to claim, and the benefit is based on care needs rather than a specific diagnosis. It is the principal cash benefit supporting older people's care needs in the UK.
programs.eligibilityNote: Generally for people over State Pension age with care/supervision needs that have usually lasted at least 6 months (waived for those who are terminally ill); usually not payable where local-authority-funded care-home care applies. NON-determinative — confirm with DWP/GOV.UK.
programs.legalBasis: Social Security Contributions and Benefits Act 1992 (s.64); Social Security (Attendance Allowance) Regulations 1991

programs.authority: Department for Work and Pensions (DWP)
programs.summary: A benefit to help with the extra costs of caring for a child under 16 who has difficulties walking or needs substantially more looking after than a child of the same age without a disability. It has a care component and a mobility component, each paid at different levels according to the child's needs. (For adults of working age, Personal Independence Payment generally applies instead, and Scotland operates the devolved Child Disability Payment.)
programs.eligibilityNote: Generally for a child under 16 with care and/or mobility needs, with residence conditions; payable whether the carer works or not. NON-determinative — confirm with DWP/GOV.UK and check the correct scheme by nation.

programs.title: Carer's Allowance
programs.authority: Department for Work and Pensions (DWP)
programs.summary: A benefit for people who regularly spend a substantial number of hours each week caring for a disabled child or adult who receives a qualifying disability benefit (such as the daily living component of Personal Independence Payment or the middle/higher care rate of Disability Living Allowance, or Attendance Allowance). The carer does not need to be related to, or live with, the person cared for. Receiving it also gives the carer National Insurance credits. It is a core part of the UK's family-and-carer support framework spanning both child and elder care.
programs.eligibilityNote: Generally requires caring at least a set minimum number of hours per week for someone receiving a qualifying benefit, with earnings and study limits for the carer. NON-determinative — confirm with DWP/GOV.UK; Scotland operates a devolved Carer Support Payment.
programs.legalBasis: Social Security Contributions and Benefits Act 1992 (s.70); Social Security (Invalid Care Allowance) Regulations 1976

programs.title: Supplementary Child Allowance / Child Supplement (Kinderzuschlag)
programs.authority: Familienkasse, Bundesagentur für Arbeit (Family Benefits Office of the Federal Employment Agency)
programs.summary: Kinderzuschlag is an income-tested supplement for low-income working parents who receive Kindergeld but whose earnings cover their own needs only barely or not the whole family's. Recipients may also gain access to education-and-participation benefits (Bildung und Teilhabe) such as subsidised school/daycare lunches and a school-supplies package, and may be exempted from child daycare (Kita) fees — making it the principal federal channel for childcare-cost relief. Applied for online (kiz-digital) or in writing to the Familienkasse.
programs.eligibilityNote: Eligibility depends on receiving Kindergeld, household income within defined minimum/maximum bands, the child's age and unmarried status, and other case factors; not determined here — confirm with the Familienkasse.
programs.legalBasis: Bundeskindergeldgesetz (BKGG) §6a; education-and-participation benefits under SGB II / Bildungs- und Teilhabepaket; Kita-fee exemption under SGB VIII

programs.title: Long-Term Care Insurance / Care Allowance (Pflegeversicherung / Pflegegeld)

programs.summary: Pflegeversicherung is Germany's mandatory social long-term care insurance, a separate branch of social insurance covering people who need long-term care (children, adults, and elders). Administered by the Pflegekassen.
programs.eligibilityNote: Eligibility and benefit form depend on an assessed care level (Pflegegrad) determined by the Medical Service / assessment body and on the chosen care arrangement; not determined here — confirm with the relevant Pflegekasse.

Through the SAAD and complementary programmes it supports personal autonomy, including services such as tele-assistance, support centres and personal-assistance benefits; recognition of the official disability degree is generally handled by the Autonomous Communities and underpins access to many disability supports.
programs.eligibilityNote: Access typically depends on residence and a recognised disability and/or dependency assessment carried out by the competent Autonomous Community; specific programmes have their own conditions. Eligibility and amounts are NOT determined here and drift — confirm with IMSERSO / the regional social-services authority.

programs.title: Universal Single Allowance for Dependent Children (Assegno Unico e Universale per i figli a carico)
programs.authority: INPS (Istituto Nazionale della Previdenza Sociale)
programs.summary: A monthly economic support paid to families for each dependent child, generally from pregnancy/birth until the child turns 21 (subject to conditions for older children), and without an age limit for dependent children with disabilities. It is 'universal' because a base amount is guaranteed to all eligible families regardless of ISEE means-test value, with the amount increasing for lower ISEE values. Since March 2022 it replaced and consolidated several prior family benefits (birth bonus, allowance for families with at least three minor children, and family allowances). Provides a recurring per-child payment; specific amounts and ISEE thresholds are revalued periodically and vary by case.
programs.eligibilityNote: Open broadly to employees, self-employed, pensioners, unemployed and non-working residents with dependent children; an ISEE filing affects the amount but not basic entitlement. Eligibility and amounts vary by case and are revalued over time — confirm with INPS.
programs.legalBasis: Decreto Legislativo 29 dicembre 2021, n. 230 (Assegno unico e universale); enabling Legge 1 aprile 2021, n. 46

programs.title: Maternity, Paternity and Parental Leave (Congedo di maternità, paternità e parentale)
programs.authority: INPS (Istituto Nazionale della Previdenza Sociale)
programs.summary: A cluster of job-protected leave entitlements with INPS-paid income replacement around childbirth and early childcare. Compulsory maternity leave covers a period around the birth (typically before and after delivery). There is also a mandatory paternity leave. Parental leave (congedo parentale) lets working parents abstain from work to care for a child during the child's early years, within statutory total limits per child and per parent, with a partial wage-replacement benefit for covered periods. Applies to childbirth and to national/international adoption or foster placement. Provides leave plus an income-replacement allowance; durations and replacement rates are set by law and updated over time.
programs.eligibilityNote: Generally for insured employees and certain self-employed/other categories meeting INPS contribution and status conditions; specific durations, replacement rates and applicable categories vary by case — confirm with INPS.
programs.legalBasis: Decreto Legislativo 26 marzo 2001, n. 151 (Testo Unico maternità/paternità); amended by D.Lgs. 105/2022 (EU Work-Life Balance Directive 2019/1158 transposition)

programs.title: Nursery Bonus / Childcare Subsidy (Bonus asilo nido e forme di supporto presso la propria abitazione)
programs.authority: INPS (Istituto Nazionale della Previdenza Sociale)
programs.summary: An INPS contribution that reimburses part of the fees for attendance at public or authorised private nurseries (asili nido) for children up to three years of age. An alternative branch provides support for at-home assistance for children under three who cannot attend nursery due to serious chronic illness (with a pediatrician's certificate). The reimbursable amount is calculated by reference to the child's date of birth and the family's ISEE for benefits for minors. Provides fee reimbursement against documented nursery payments; maximum amounts and ISEE-based tiers are set annually and vary by case.
programs.eligibilityNote: For parents of children aged 0–3 (including adopted children), who are Italian citizens or legally resident foreigners; the amount depends on ISEE for minors. Eligibility, thresholds and maximum amounts vary by case and drift — confirm with INPS.
programs.legalBasis: Article 1, paragraph 355, Legge 11 dicembre 2016, n. 232 (Legge di bilancio 2017), as subsequently amended

programs.title: Accompaniment Allowance for Civil Invalids (Indennità di accompagnamento agli invalidi civili)
programs.authority: INPS (Istituto Nazionale della Previdenza Sociale)
programs.summary: A cash benefit for people recognised as 100% civil invalids (physical or mental) who are in a condition of non-self-sufficiency in daily life — unable to walk without the help of an assistant or unable to perform daily living activities (eating, dressing, personal hygiene). It is granted on the basis of a health/medical-commission assessment, is independent of personal income and age, and is intended to help meet the cost of constant assistance. Paid as a recurring monthly amount following medical recognition; payment may be suspended during prolonged fully State-funded hospitalisation.
programs.eligibilityNote: For persons meeting the health (100% non-self-sufficiency) requirements and stably resident in Italy, regardless of income or age; requires medical-commission assessment. Eligibility and amounts vary by case and are revalued — confirm with INPS.
programs.legalBasis: Legge 11 febbraio 1980, n. 18 (indennità di accompagnamento); related Legge 5 febbraio 1992, n. 104

programs.title: Universal Benefit for Non-Self-Sufficient Elderly (Prestazione Universale per anziani non autosufficienti — Decreto Anziani)
programs.authority: INPS (Istituto Nazionale della Previdenza Sociale)
programs.summary: An experimental benefit for very elderly, non-self-sufficient people with a 'very serious' assessed assistance need, introduced under the 'Decreto Anziani' reform. It is intended to strengthen home-based care and personal autonomy and combines a cash component with provision oriented to assistance/care services. Where granted, it is coordinated with and absorbs the existing accompaniment allowance and certain regionally provided benefits. The benefit runs as an experimental measure over a defined period and targets a high-need elder population assessed by INPS.
programs.eligibilityNote: Targeted at people of advanced age (high age threshold), with a very serious assessed assistance need, a social-health ISEE below a set ceiling, and entitlement to the accompaniment allowance; this is an experimental, time-limited measure. Eligibility, thresholds, amounts and the experimental window vary by case and drift — confirm with INPS.
programs.legalBasis: Decreto Legislativo 15 marzo 2024, n. 29 (Decreto Anziani), enabling Legge 23 marzo 2023, n. 33

programs.summary: Poland's universal child-rearing benefit ('Rodzina 800 plus'), a monthly cash benefit paid per child to help with the costs of raising children up to age 18. programs.authority: Zakład Ubezpieczeń Społecznych (ZUS) / Ministry of Family, Labour and Social Policy; need-for-support assessment by Provincial Disability Assessment Teams (WZON)
programs.summary: A benefit paid directly to an adult person with a disability (rather than to their carer) that came into force on 1 January 2024. The amount is tied to a 'need-for-support' point assessment carried out by Provincial Disability Assessment Teams (WZON) and is expressed as a percentage of the social pension. Unlike older carer benefits, it allows the recipient and their carer to remain in paid work.

programs.title: Family Tax Benefit (FTB)
programs.authority: Services Australia (Centrelink), Australian Government
programs.summary: A two-part means-tested payment that helps eligible families with the cost of raising dependent children. Part A is paid per eligible child; Part B provides extra assistance to single-parent and single-income families. Generally paid fortnightly with end-of-financial-year balancing. Administered by Services Australia (Centrelink) and claimed via myGov.
programs.eligibilityNote: Broadly for families with a dependent child (typically 15 or younger, or a full-time secondary student 16–19 not receiving certain payments) who care for the child at least 35% of the time and meet income and residence tests. Eligibility, rates, and income thresholds vary by case and change over time — confirm with Services Australia.
programs.legalBasis: A New Tax System (Family Assistance) Act 1999 (Cth)

programs.title: Parental Leave Pay (Paid Parental Leave scheme)
programs.authority: Services Australia (Centrelink) + Department of Social Services, Australian Government
programs.summary: A government payment under the Paid Parental Leave scheme that helps eligible working parents take time off to care for a newborn or recently adopted child. The entitlement is a number of weeks of pay that can be shared as a family, and recent changes added a superannuation contribution on the payment. Distinct from any employer-provided paid parental leave and from the unpaid parental leave entitlement under the Fair Work framework.
programs.eligibilityNote: Broadly for the carer of a child born or adopted, subject to work, income, and residence tests, and timing rules. Number of weeks, rate, and rules depend on the child's birth/adoption date and change over time — confirm with Services Australia.
programs.legalBasis: Paid Parental Leave Act 2010 (Cth)

programs.title: Child Care Subsidy (CCS)
programs.authority: Services Australia (Centrelink) + Department of Education, Australian Government
programs.summary: The main government subsidy reducing the cost of approved child care. The subsidy is generally paid directly to the approved child care provider to lower the fees families pay; families pay the remaining gap fee. The subsidy percentage depends on family income, with possible higher rates for families with more than one young child in care. Claimed via myGov.
programs.eligibilityNote: Broadly for families caring for a child aged 13 or younger not attending secondary school (with exceptions) who use an approved child care service and meet residence and immunisation requirements. programs.legalBasis: A New Tax System (Family Assistance) Act 1999 (Cth); administered under the Family Assistance framework

programs.title: Support at Home program / My Aged Care
programs.authority: Department of Health, Disability and Ageing (My Aged Care), Australian Government
programs.summary: The national entry point and government-funded in-home aged care program helping older people remain living independently at home. Support at Home funds services, products, equipment and home modifications — including clinical care (e.g. nursing), help with daily independence (dressing, medications, transport, respite), and everyday-living support (cleaning, shopping, meal preparation). My Aged Care is the access point for assessment and finding approved providers.
programs.eligibilityNote: Broadly for older people (and others meeting aged-care criteria) following an aged-care assessment arranged through My Aged Care. Contributions, caps and service rules apply and change over time — confirm with My Aged Care / the Department of Health, Disability and Ageing.
programs.legalBasis: Aged Care Act (Cth) framework (program reforms commenced 1 November 2025, replacing the Home Care Packages and Short-Term Restorative Care programs)

programs.title: National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS)
programs.authority: National Disability Insurance Agency (NDIA), Australian Government
programs.summary: A national scheme providing individualised funding to eligible people with permanent and significant disability for supports that build independence and participation in everyday life. Participants receive an individual plan describing needed supports and funding, choose their providers, and use plan funding to pay them. The NDIS is administered by the independent National Disability Insurance Agency (NDIA).

programs.title: Carer Payment and Carer Allowance
programs.authority: Services Australia (Centrelink), Australian Government
programs.summary: Two government payments supporting people who care for someone with disability, a severe medical condition, or who is frail aged. programs.title: Working for Families (incl. Family Tax Credit & In-Work Tax Credit)
programs.authority: Inland Revenue (Te Tari Taake) — administered jointly with Work and Income (Te Hiranga Tangata)
programs.summary: Working for Families is New Zealand's principal package of payments to help families with the day-to-day costs of raising dependent children. Its core components include the Family Tax Credit (a per-child payment for caregivers of dependent children), the In-Work Tax Credit (for working families), the Minimum Family Tax Credit (a top-up to a minimum after-tax income for working families), and Best Start (a payment for the early years of a child's life). Payments can be received weekly, fortnightly, or as a lump sum after the tax year ends.
programs.eligibilityNote: Generally for principal caregivers of financially dependent children who are NZ tax residents; entitlement depends on number of children, family income, and work status. Components, thresholds, and amounts are income-tested and change over time — eligibility and amounts are not determined here and must be confirmed with Inland Revenue / Work and Income.
programs.legalBasis: Income Tax Act 2007 (Working for Families tax credits); administered under Tax Administration Act 1994

programs.title: Disability Allowance & Child Disability Allowance
programs.authority: Work and Income (Te Hiranga Tangata), Ministry of Social Development
programs.summary: The Disability Allowance is a weekly payment for people (including dependent children applied for by a caregiver) who have regular, ongoing additional costs because of a disability — for example certain medical, equipment, travel, or care-related costs. The Child Disability Allowance is a separate, non-income-tested, non-taxable payment for the main carer of a child or young person who has a serious disability requiring constant care and attention. Both may be payable for the same child.
programs.eligibilityNote: Disability Allowance requires verified ongoing additional disability-related costs and may be income-tested; Child Disability Allowance requires a medical certificate confirming a child needs significantly more care/attention than others of the same age and is not income- or asset-tested. programs.title: Family Hope Program (Program Keluarga Harapan / PKH)
programs.authority: Kementerian Sosial Republik Indonesia (Ministry of Social Affairs / Kemensos)
programs.summary: Indonesia's flagship conditional cash transfer (CCT) program for poor and vulnerable families, launched in 2007. It provides periodic cash assistance to eligible families conditional on accessing health and education services, with benefit components tied to household members in priority categories including pregnant women, young children (early childhood), school-age children, persons with severe disabilities, and the elderly. Aims to reduce household expenditure burden and invest in human capital across generations. Disbursed via the banking/postal system to registered beneficiary families.
programs.eligibilityNote: Targets registered poor/vulnerable families (DTKS social registry) with members in priority categories and conditional service compliance; eligibility, components, and amounts vary by case and over time and must be confirmed with Kemensos.
programs.legalBasis: Administered by Kementerian Sosial under social-welfare and poverty-reduction regulations (Permensos on PKH); supervised with Bappenas.

programs.title: Child Support Grant (CSG)
programs.authority: South African Social Security Agency (SASSA), Department of Social Development
programs.summary: A monthly cash social grant paid to the primary caregiver of a child to help meet the child's basic needs. Introduced in 1998 to replace the former State Maintenance Grant, it is South Africa's largest social grant by reach and targets lower-income households caring for children. A 'top-up' amount is available to relatives or non-parent primary caregivers caring for orphaned children. Applications are free and made at SASSA offices or via the SASSA services portal.
programs.eligibilityNote: Generally for South African citizens / permanent residents / refugees who are the child's primary caregiver, subject to an income (means) test, with the child below the qualifying age. Thresholds, ages and amounts vary by case and change over time. Eligibility and current amounts must be confirmed directly with SASSA; this is not a determination.
programs.legalBasis: Social Assistance Act 13 of 2004 (and regulations); administered by SASSA under the South African Social Security Agency Act 9 of 2004

programs.summary: Short-term income replacement paid from the Unemployment Insurance Fund to qualifying employed contributors who take maternity, parental, or adoption leave (as well as for unemployment and illness). programs.title: Foster Child Grant (Foster Care Grant)
programs.authority: South African Social Security Agency (SASSA), Department of Social Development
programs.summary: A monthly cash social grant paid to foster parents to provide for the basic needs of a child who has been formally placed in their care by a Children's Court. It supports court-ordered foster placements of children up to the qualifying age and is distinct from (and may be combined with the Care Dependency Grant for) a severely disabled foster child. Applications are made at SASSA offices and require the court foster-placement order.
programs.eligibilityNote: Generally for foster parents who have a valid Children's Court order placing the child in their care, with the child below the qualifying age. There is no means test on the foster parent, but the court order and other conditions apply; rules and amounts vary by case and change over time — confirm directly with SASSA; this is not a determination.
programs.legalBasis: Social Assistance Act 13 of 2004 (and regulations); foster placement under the Children's Act 38 of 2005; administered by SASSA under the South African Social Security Agency Act 9 of 2004

programs.title: Cash Transfer for Orphans and Vulnerable Children (CT-OVC) / Inua Jamii — Mpango wa Pesa kwa Mayatima na Watoto Walio Hatarini
programs.authority: Ministry of Labour and Social Protection / State Department for Social Protection and Senior Citizens Affairs (under the national Inua Jamii National Safety Net Programme); child-services component administered via the State Department for Children Services
programs.summary: A regular government cash transfer paid to extremely poor households caring for orphans and vulnerable children. The programme is designed to encourage the fostering and retention of orphans and vulnerable children within their families and communities and to promote their human-capital development — supporting school enrolment and retention, improved health and nutrition, household food security, and child protection. It is one of the four cash-transfer streams consolidated under the national Inua Jamii safety-net programme.
programs.eligibilityNote: Generally aimed at extremely poor households that include one or more orphans or vulnerable children as permanent members, with a Kenyan-citizen caregiver, not concurrently receiving other government cash transfers (with limited exceptions). This is non-determinative; eligibility, enrolment status and any payment must be confirmed directly with the Ministry / State Department for Children Services.
programs.legalBasis: Constitution of Kenya (2010), Art. 43 (economic and social rights) and Art. 53 (children's rights); Kenya National Social Protection Policy; Children Act, 2022

programs.title: Statutory Maternity and Paternity Leave — Likizo ya Uzazi (Kisheria), Employment Act 2007
programs.authority: Ministry of Labour and Social Protection, Republic of Kenya (statutory employment right; enforced via the labour framework / Employment and Labour Relations Court)
programs.summary: A statutory employment entitlement under the Employment Act, 2007. Female employees are entitled to fully paid maternity leave (three months / 90 calendar days) and male employees to fully paid paternity leave (two weeks / 14 calendar days) following the birth of a child. The law protects employees from dismissal or penalty for taking parental leave and preserves other leave entitlements. It is an employer-provided, legally mandated benefit rather than a state cash transfer.
programs.eligibilityNote: Generally applies to employees in formal employment relationships, subject to notice and other conditions in the Act. This is non-determinative; current durations, conditions and entitlements must be confirmed against the Employment Act as amended and with the Ministry of Labour or qualified counsel.

programs.title: EESSI — Electronic Exchange of Social Security Information
programs.authority: European Commission (Directorate-General for Employment, Social Affairs and Inclusion)
programs.summary: Decentralised IT system that lets national social-security institutions across participating countries exchange information securely and faster, as required by EU social-security coordination rules. It underpins cross-border handling of benefit branches including family benefits, sickness/maternity, invalidity (disability) and old-age/long-term care, interconnecting thousands of institutions across the EU plus Iceland, Liechtenstein, Norway, Switzerland and the UK. hagukumi references it as the institutional plumbing behind cross-border care/family-benefit coordination, not as a benefit itself.
programs.eligibilityNote: Back-office institutional system, not a citizen-facing benefit; individuals do not apply to EESSI directly. Apply via the competent national institution and confirm entitlement there.
programs.legalBasis: Regulation (EC) No 883/2004 and Regulation (EC) No 987/2009 (digitalisation of social-security coordination)
